extensionBoundaries.name: Policy rule packs
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Custom policy rule definitions that feed into the standard policy evaluation contract.
expectedOutputs: Policy requirement results mapped to the Product Core contract
extensionBoundaries.notes: Product Core provides the evaluation engine; rule packs are injected at runtime.

extensionBoundaries.name: Evidence source adapters
extensionBoundaries.purpose: External evidence feeds mapped to the canonical evidence bundle shape.
extensionBoundaries.coreContract: Evidence bundle records and evidence hash records
extensionBoundaries.notes: Adapters must normalise external data into the flat, deterministic bundle format.

extensionBoundaries.name: Evidence verification strategies
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Alternative or additional verification methods that still produce a standard verification result record.
expectedOutputs: Verification result record (valid / tampered / missing / error)
extensionBoundaries.notes: Product Core uses SHA-256 hash recomparison by default; additional strategies may layer on top.

extensionBoundaries.name: Auditor export formats
extensionBoundaries.purpose: New output formats that still export the canonical auditor export record structure.
expectedOutputs: Serialized auditor export in the target format (JSON, PDF, XML, etc.)
extensionBoundaries.notes: Format adapters should not alter the canonical record structure.

extensionBoundaries.name: Compliance readiness dimensions
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Additional readiness dimensions that feed into the standard readiness summary.
extensionBoundaries.notes: Dimensions may be added without changing the summary contract.

extensionBoundaries.name: Permission profiles
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Optional profile definitions evaluated within the standard permission contract.
expectedInputs: Profile definitions with scopes and role mappings
extensionBoundaries.notes: Product Core evaluator is stable; profiles are configuration, not code changes.

extensionBoundaries.name: Approval workflow profiles
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Optional workflow patterns that still produce standard approval lifecycle records.
expectedOutputs: Lifecycle state transitions and final approval / denial record
extensionBoundaries.notes: Profiles may vary by organisation; the transition record remains canonical.

extensionBoundaries.name: Storage / persistence backends
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Replaceable storage implementations that preserve Product Core IDs and integrity constraints.
extensionBoundaries.coreContract: Persistent IDs across all Product Core entities
extensionBoundaries.notes: SQLite is the current default; PostgreSQL is optional. Both are behind the persistence abstraction.

extensionBoundaries.name: Auth / identity providers
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Replaceable identity and authentication backends that still produce standard operator references and role assignments.
expectedOutputs: Authenticated operator reference with role mapping
extensionBoundaries.notes: Current auth is local-token only; future providers must map into the operator role model.

extensionBoundaries.name: SDK / API clients
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Generated or handwritten clients that speak the stable API contract.
expectedOutputs: Valid API requests and parsed responses
extensionBoundaries.notes: Clients are external to the Product Core and must track contract versions.

extensionBoundaries.name: Workflow orchestrator integrations
extensionBoundaries.purpose: External orchestrators that interact via the stable API contract.
expectedOutputs: API calls conforming to the OpenAPI contract
extensionBoundaries.notes: Orchestrators are consumers, not extensions, of the Product Core.

extensionBoundaries.name: Observability sinks
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Metrics, logging, and alerting backends that consume standard event streams.
extensionBoundaries.notes: Sinks must not alter Product Core event structure.

extensionBoundaries.name: Optional blockchain anchoring
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Periodic anchoring of integrity hashes to a blockchain for enhanced tamper evidence.
expectedOutputs: Anchoring receipts linked by execution or bundle ID
extensionBoundaries.notes: Must remain optional; Product Core must not depend on blockchain availability.

extensionBoundaries.name: Optional wallet / payment integration
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Wallet signatures, disbursement records, or payment receipts linked to grant executions via stable IDs.
extensionBoundaries.coreContract: Grant execution records and stable IDs
expectedOutputs: Receipts linked to executions by Product Core IDs
extensionBoundaries.notes: Must remain optional; Product Core must not depend on payment infrastructure.

extensionBoundaries.name: Dashboard / UI layer
extensionBoundaries.purpose: Client applications that consume the stable API contract without embedding Product Core logic.
expectedOutputs: Rendered UI state and API calls
extensionBoundaries.notes: UI is a consumer, not a dependency, of the Product Core.

adapterRules: Adapters must not redefine Product Core records.
Adapters must map into stable Product Core contracts.
Adapters should not leak secrets into records or examples.
Adapters should keep sensitive data off-chain.
Adapters should fail closed where appropriate.
Adapters should be tested independently from Product Core logic.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Payment processing
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Not required for grant verification or audit records.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Wallet custody
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Financial custody is outside the verification layer.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Blockchain dependency
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Currently optional; Product Core must remain independent.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Public grant discovery / matchmaking
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Discovery is a separate product surface.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Full SaaS tenant management
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Single-tenant or self-hosted is the current assumption.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Production dashboard
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: UI is an extension; the API is the Product Core.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Legal / compliance certification
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Structured records support audit workflows but do not replace legal review.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: HSM-backed signing implementation
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Software signing is acceptable for pilots; HSM is a future upgrade path.

nonCoreBoundaries.name: Hosted production deployment automation
nonCoreBoundaries.rationale: Manual deployment documentation is sufficient for the first pilot.

futureIntegrationCandidates: Third-party evidence vaults (cloud object storage, document management)
testingExpectations: Adapter tests should run without full Product Core fixtures.
Boundary tests should verify mapping into stable contracts.
Replacement tests should confirm interchangeability of two adapter implementations.
Contract regression tests should flag changes to Product Core record shapes.
securityExpectations: Adapters must sanitize inputs before mapping to Product Core.
Adapters must not expose internal identifiers in external-facing output.
Failure in an adapter must not corrupt Product Core state.
Secrets must not be logged, stored in records, or returned in API responses.
